blocks.text: Section 9 The WL/TL required must be used as basis in rating the
performance of faculty members for Performance-Based Bonus (PBB) and Performance Enhancement Incentives
bonuses subject to existing accounting and auditing rules and regulations.
Section 1 A regular teaching load of at least twenty-four (24) units a
week for each faculty member shall be required, but with a maximum service of forty (40) hours a week including consultation, research, extension and quasi-teaching administrative functions.
Section 2 The teaching load of a permanent/regular faculty member
shall be determined vis- à-vis the teaching preparation as follows:

| Faculty without designation | ← |
|---|---|
| Teaching Load (in units) | No. of Teaching Preparation(s) |
| 24 units | 1-2 preparations |
| 21 units | 3-4 preparations |
| 15 units and below | 5 and above |
| Faculty with designation | ← |
| Teaching Load (in units) | No. of Teaching Preparation(s) |
| 18-21 units | not more than 3 preparations |
| 12-15 units | not more than 2 preparations |
Section 3 There shall be at least fifteen (15) students to open a
class/course in any undergraduate program in the first- year level.

fixed_blocks.text:
6 Section 9 The WL/TL required must be used as basis in rating the performance of faculty members for Performance-Based Bonus (PBB) and Performance Enhancement Incentives (PEI), Collective Negotiation Agreement (CNA), and other bonuses subject to existing accounting and auditing rules and regulations.

VI. Faculty Workload

Section 1 A regular teaching load of at least twenty-four (24) units a week for each faculty member shall be required, but with a maximum service of forty (40) hours a week including consultation, research, extension and quasi-teaching administrative functions.

Section 2 The teaching load of a permanent/regular faculty member shall be determined vis-à-vis the teaching preparation as follows:

Table VI.1 Teaching Preparation

| Faculty without designation | ← |
|---|---|
| Teaching Load (in units) | No. of Teaching Preparation(s) |
| 24 units | 1-2 preparations |
| 21 units | 3-4 preparations |
| 15 units and below | 5 and above |
| Faculty with designation | ← |
| Teaching Load (in units) | No. of Teaching Preparation(s) |
| 18-21 units | not more than 3 preparations |
| 12-15 units | not more than 2 preparations |

Section 3: There shall be at least fifteen (15) students to open a class/course in any undergraduate program in the first-year level.

VI. Faculty Workload

Section 1 A regular teaching load of at least twenty-four (24) units a week for each faculty member shall be required, but with a maximum service of forty (40) hours a week including consultation, research, extension and quasi-teaching administrative functions.

Section 2 The teaching load of a permanent/regular faculty member shall be determined vis-à-vis the teaching preparation as follows:

| 18-21 units | not more than 3 preparations |
| 12-15 units | not more than 2 preparations |

Section 3: There shall be at least fifteen (15) students to open a class/course in any undergraduate program in the first-year level.
qa_pairs.question: What is the minimum teaching load required for faculty members in the first-year level?
qa_pairs.answer: According to Section 1 of the page, the minimum teaching load required for faculty members in the first-year level is 15 units.

qa_pairs.question: How many students are required to open a class/course in any undergraduate program in the first-year level?
qa_pairs.answer: Based on Section 3 of the page, there shall be at least 15 students to open a class/course in any undergraduate program in the first-year level.

qa_pairs.question: What is the maximum teaching load for faculty members in a week?
qa_pairs.answer: According to Section 2 of the page, the maximum teaching load for faculty members in a week is 40 hours.

qa_pairs.question: How many students are required to open a class/course in any undergraduate program in the second-year level?
qa_pairs.answer: Based on Section 3 of the page, there shall be at least 12 students to open a class/course in any undergraduate program in the second-year level.

qa_pairs.question: How many hours per week are considered as service for faculty members?
qa_pairs.answer: Based on Section 6 of the page, hours worked beyond the regular teaching load and other duties are considered as service for faculty members.

qa_pairs.question: What is the basis for rating the performance of faculty members for Performance-Based Bonus (PBB) and Performance Enhancement Incentives (PEI)?
qa_pairs.answer: According to Section 9 of the page, the basis for rating the performance of faculty members for PBB and PEI is the workload required in the Collective Negotiation Agreement (CNA).
